channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Windsor to Relocate Canada Geese to Manage Population Growth
item.description: In Windsor, Ontario, local authorities are taking steps to address the growing population of Canada geese as part of a newly expanded wildlife management program. The initiative aims to mitigate the e...

item.title: Government Defers to Ombudsman and DOJ on Publication of ICI Report on Flood Control Projects
item.description: Malacañang announced on Monday that it will leave the decision to the Office of the Ombudsman and the Department of Justice (DOJ) regarding whether to release the final report of the Independent Commi...

item.title: Toys ‘R’ Us Canada defends trademarks amid financial struggles and asset sale efforts
item.description: Toys “R” Us Canada is engaged in several trademark disputes while under creditor protection and attempting to sell off assets to ease financial strain. The toy retailer, which owes at least $120 milli...

item.title: Drizzle Hits Parts of Karachi as Rain Expected Across City
item.description: KARACHI: Residents of Karachi experienced light drizzle in various parts of the city on Monday, signalling the start of changing weather patterns, according to the Pakistan Meteorological Department (...

item.title: Ruto navigates coalition talks with ODM ahead of 2027 elections
item.description: As the 2027 elections approach, President Ruto is engaging in sensitive coalition talks with the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM), which is staking claim to the deputy presidency and other key positio...

item.title: SA Celebrities Share Diverse Easter Celebrations
item.description: South African celebrities marked the Easter weekend with a varied mix of celebrations, ranging from solo reflection to lively family gatherings. Rachel Kolisi, the former wife of Springbok captain Siy...

item.title: Riniki Bhuyan Sharma refutes Congress claims on foreign assets and passports, challenges Gaurav Gogoi over alleged bank accounts
item.description: Assam chief minister Himanta Biswa Sarma’s wife, Riniki Bhuyan Sharma, has firmly denied Congress accusations that she holds multiple foreign passports and undisclosed assets abroad. Responding to all...

item.title: Suspended Ekurhuleni Metro Police Deputy Chief to Testify at Madlanga Commission
item.description: The Madlanga Commission of Inquiry, led by Justice Mbuyiseli Madlanga, is set to resume hearings after a brief Easter break. The Commission has already heard from 49 witnesses over 84 days, investigat...

item.title: Trump's statements on Iran conflict fail to stabilize markets amid rising oil and gas prices
item.description: Despite President Donald Trump's recent address intended to reassure investors and voters about the U.S. and Israel's conflict with Iran, financial markets have remained highly unstable. In his primet...

item.title: Police probe after man violates security at Delhi Assembly and leaves bouquet near Speaker’s car
item.description: A major security lapse occurred at the Delhi Legislative Assembly complex on Monday afternoon when a masked man driving a white Tata Sierra SUV with Uttar Pradesh registration forcefully entered throu...

item.title: Supreme Court directs NCLAT to decide on Vedanta's plea against Adani takeover of Jaypee assets
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to intervene in a National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) decision which allowed Adani Enterprises to acquire the assets of the insolvent Jaypee Group. The...

item.title: A Selection of Cartoons for April 7, 2026
item.description: This article offers a selection of cartoons from April 7, 2026, providing a light-hearted commentary on the political climate in Australia and around the world. The cartoons aim to provide a humorous ...

item.title: DR Congo agrees to temporarily accept third-country deportees from United States
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo government has announced a new temporary agreement with the United States under President Donald Trump’s administration. According to the statement released on Sunday,...

item.title: Arizona hiker hospitalized after multiple bee stings amid increased swarm activity
item.description: An Arizona hiker is in critical condition after suffering more than one hundred bee stings while on Lookout Mountain near Phoenix. The man called emergency services for help when he was unable to desc...

item.title: Religious Leaders in Tanzania Urge Unity and Peace During Easter Celebrations
item.description: Across Tanzania, Christian leaders used Easter celebrations to call for peace, unity and love among citizens. During the National Easter Mass at St Alban’s Anglican Cathedral in Dar es Salaam, Bishop ...

item.title: Building Lasting Friendships Through Sincerity and Trust
item.description: This article discusses the crucial role of sincerity and trust in forming enduring friendships and fostering unity within communities. Genuine friendships rely on being truthful, kind, and trustworthy...

item.title: KGL Resources weighs refining options for Jervois copper project amid Mt Isa smelter challenges
item.description: Australia’s next major copper development, led by KGL Resources, could choose to refine its output outside of Glencore’s Mt Isa smelter, signalling a possible shift in how new mining projects handle d...

item.title: Stunning Flamingo Formations Spotted Over Kutch
item.description: A breathtaking spectacle unfolded over the Rann of Kutch, Gujarat, as a large flock of flamingos created stunning and intricate formations in the sky. The aerial display, captured in video footage, sh...

item.title: Civil groups in Indonesia file genocide case against Myanmar’s president over Rohingya persecution
item.description: A group of civil society organisations in Indonesia has filed a criminal complaint against Myanmar’s newly elected president, Min Aung Hlaing, accusing him of committing genocide against the Rohingya ...

item.title: West Bengal Tribunal Restores Congress Candidate's Voter Status After EC Fails to Justify Deletion
item.description: In a significant decision by the Appellate Tribunal in Kolkata, Motab Shaikh, the Congress candidate from Farakka, was reinstated as a valid voter ahead of the upcoming West Bengal Assembly elections....
